name: ESP32 CSI Toolkit
snippet: The ESP32 CSI Toolkit provides researchers access to Channel State Information (CSI) directly from the ESP32 microcontroller.

name: StevenMHernandez/ESP32-CSI-Tool: Extract Channel State Information from WiFi  - GitHub
snippet: The purpose of this project is to allow for the collection of Channel State Information (CSI) from the ESP32 Wi-Fi enabled microcontroller.

name: Introduction to ESP-CSI Solution - - — ESP-Techpedia latest documentation
snippet: ESP-CSI (Channel State Information) is a technology provided by Espressif for obtaining the state information of Wi-Fi channels.

name: ESP-CSI: DIY WiFi Human Presence Detection - Hackster.io
snippet: I turned Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32s into a human detector: ESP-CSI is an incredible hack, but too finicky and power-hungry to replace radar.

name: Wi-Fi Sensing Tool Release: Gathering 802.11ax Channel State Information from a Commercial Wi-Fi Access Point - arXiv
snippet: The currently available and most widely used open-source CSI extraction tools includes CSITool [1] , Nexmon CSI Extractor [2] , Atheros CSI Tool ...

name: Just experimenting with ESP-CSI (human presence detection) and wonder if anyone has any hints? : r/esp32 - Reddit
snippet: A device is able to sense presence and movement just by watching the variations in the wireless channels, it makes a project I got fed up with viable again.

name: jonathanmuller/ESP32-gather-channel-state-information-CSI-: Get CSI frames (Channel State Information) with the use of an ESP32 WiFi chip - GitHub

name: A Low-cost ESP32-driven Wireless Key Generation System Based on Response Mechanism
snippet: Since Channel State Information (CSI) is a fine-grained characterization of channel information, there are currently many studies using CSI for key generation.

name: How can I get CSI (channel state information) from my own ESP32 Chip?
snippet: I just follow the WiFi documentation wifi.rst in esp-idf directory to modify the given example "station" to get channel state information. It works well. Missing: tool | Show results with: tool
